spec_match.rationale: The menu bar now uses focusgroup="menubar" with nested focusgroup="menu" submenus, matching the current behavior-token model.

interactive.rationale: The invalid nested-button markup was replaced with sibling menu popups and active open/close handlers, but keyboard behavior was not verified through chrome-devtools-mcp.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page copy and comparison labels now identify menubar as the behavior token instead of presenting bare wrap as the focusgroup definition.

edge_cases.rationale: Nested menus and Escape handling are present, but arrow wrapping and submenu menu focus need live browser verification.

references.rationale: The page retains the required ChromeStatus reference link.

design_system.rationale: The flat bordered menu, log, and comparison panels continue to use shared CSS variables and explicit button text colors.

openQuestions.title: Verify menubar and submenu focusgroups
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp on /v150/focusgroup/menu-bar/ to Tab into the menubar, move with Left/Right, open each submenu with Enter/Space, verify nested Up/Down menu movement and Escape return, and inspect console/network.

summary: Static repair changed the bare focusgroup="wrap" menu bar to current menubar/menu tokens and fixed invalid nested button markup; DevTools-MCP verification remains pending.
